title: Class 2 to Class 1 low-risk confirmation transition scenario
description: Deterministic scenario skeleton for a Class 2 clarification path where the user or caregiver selects a bounded low-risk lighting candidate. The runtime re-enters the Deterministic Validator with the confirmed bounded candidate and still requires validator approval before Class 1 bounded actuation. The selection itself is not a Policy Router re-routing event.

steps.description: Publish an ambiguous but non-emergency context payload that cannot safely resolve the target device without clarification.
Route to Class 2 clarification. Candidate generation may be used for bounded guidance only.
Publish bounded candidate choices to safe_deferral/clarification/interaction. The payload is evidence only and not actuation authorization.
Collect user or caregiver selection of a low-risk lighting candidate. The selection does not directly authorize actuation.

Re-enter the Deterministic Validator with the confirmed bounded candidate (action_hint + target_hint from the selected candidate) and require validator approval against the canonical low-risk lighting catalog before any Class 1 dispatch. The Policy Router is not invoked again.
Observe audit evidence for candidate presentation, selection, validator re-entry on the confirmed candidate, validator approval, and bounded Class 1 outcome.

transition_outcomes.condition: User or caregiver confirms a bounded lighting candidate and the Deterministic Validator approves the confirmed candidate on validator re-entry. The selection itself is not a Policy Router re-routing event.

notes: This scenario proves that a Class 2 selection does not bypass Deterministic Validator approval; the confirmed bounded candidate is re-validated against the canonical low-risk catalog before any Class 1 dispatch.
Candidate text alone does not authorize actuation.
Only canonical low-risk lighting actions may be admitted for Class 1 transition.
Doorlock must not be treated as a Class 1 low-risk action.
